entry.title: Re: IAS/IPS: UPN in Azure and UserName in Cloud Apps
entry.content:
{ "sourcePath": "$.userPrincipalName", "targetPath": "$.loginName", "correlationAttribute": true },

To Build Transformation between Source & Target Systems

JSON Path will save your time and make you better to build.. Paste your JSON payload from source system and identify the path to update in your Transformation to Target Systems fields. Kindly refer the below blog which will speed your build.

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/understanding-of-sap-ips-transformation-logic-to-map-source-amp-target/ba-p/13549783
  • https://jsonpath.com

    https://github.com/yogananda-muthaiah/SAP-Sucessfactors-Incentive-Management/tree/main/Integrations/IPS

entry.title: Re: Issue with Automatic Cancellation of Purchase Invoice in Intercompany Billing
entry.content:
Hi,

The sales and purchasing processes are independent,

so the cancellation of the sales invoice will naturally not trigger the cancellation of the purchase invoice.

entry.title: Re: Today is ROCKY DAY in the city of brotherly love! (What is your favorite Rocky movie??)
entry.content:
Wow, I cannot believe the original release was in 1976! Crazy!
And, how did you celebrate @StephanieMarley ? Watched a Rocky movie? 😊

entry.title: BW reporting preview freezes in Eclipse
entry.content:
Hi all,

since a while we have the phenomenon that if one tries to open a value help of a query variable or just wants to click to the next page of a result (without vars) in the preview, the preview window just freezes - the little animated "wheel" as well

We are using the latest Eclipse (2024-09) and tools from https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/

anyone else with this issue?
any ideas appreciated...

thx a lot in advance!
Mathias

entry.title: Re: Content 'Not mobile accessible' for non-mobiles (Tabl...

entry.title: Truncate table and mass upload with CAP
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I want to replicate a table with CAP. The entire table is to be “copied” with each run. This means that I first have to delete all entries from the target table and then write all rows from the source table to the target table.

If I do this with the standard CRUD operations, it takes quite a long time (there are approx. 165000 rows in the table).

Is there a possibility to do a “TRUNCATE” on the DB table and then a “mass upload”?

Many thanks and best regards
Heinrich

entry.title: Re: Eclipse - Where-Used functionality for ABAP CDS views not working
entry.content: Please explain what "your colleague" does to get more than 200 entries. Why is there a difference with you? What ABAP version do you use? NB: installing the latest Eclipse ADT version is often not sufficient, what is important is the backend ABAP version and SP number.

entry.title: Re: Eclipse - Where-Used functionality for ABAP CDS views not working
entry.content:
He is on a different system but same version. It is an S/4HANA 2023 SP02 Backend.
Also tried on a different backend-system with same result. My guess is that it is more related to an eclipse issue.

entry.title: Copy Purchase Requisition(PR) price to Purchase Order(PO)
entry.content:
In SAP Materials Management (MM), handling material prices from Purchase Requisition (PR) to Purchase Order (PO) is crucial for maintaining accurate procurement costs. This process can vary based on different scenarios and configurations. Let's explore how material prices are managed and delve into specific test cases.

When a Purchase Requisition (PR) is created, it may include a suggested price for the material. This price can be derived from various sources such as the material master, purchasing info records, or manual entry. When converting a PR to a Purchase Order (PO), the system determines the final price based on predefined rules and configurations.

Test case 1: Adopt PR price to PO

Pre-Requisite configuration

SPRO > IMG > Material Management > Purchasing > Authorization Management > Define Functional
Authorization for Buyers > Function Authorization: Purchase Order.

Create a parameter and activate "Adopt PO Price"

Assign the parameter value to the parameter EFB in Transaction SU01 under parameters tab.

Material Price

Create a PR 

In valuation tab you can update PO price as "As Gross Price"

(you can make this option as default setting by chosing personal setting)

Create a PO by refering PR

Material price for: 50 SAR 1 EA

Net value in PO: 100 SAR for 2 EA (Price adopted from Material > PR > PO)

Test case 2: Material Line Item Added Directly in PO

When a material line item is added directly in the PO, the system will fetch the price from the purchasing info record.

Expected Outcome: The price in the PO should reflect the price from the purchasing info record.

Test Case 3: Preventing Price Copy from PR to PO

In some cases, you might not want the price from the PR to be copied to the PO. This can be achieved by adjusting the variant parameters or selecting the "Do not apply" option in the PR item details.

Expected Outcome: The price in the PO should be derived from the purchasing info record or manually, not the PR.

 

By following the outlined test cases, you can ensure that the correct prices are applied in different scenarios, maintaining accuracy and consistency in your procurement process.

entry.title: Re: Flexible workflow: Recipients cannot be edited (The r...
entry.content:
Hi Javo,

Recipients in workitem can be modified or recalculated (SWIA or from the Fiori app Flexible Workflow administration), the recipient for work item notifications can't be easily modified.

 

Regards

 

JCD

entry.title: Re: SAC Planning - Actuals Vs Plan - Variance column formula
entry.content:
@SarathSunny1 

Its such a simple requirement but we still cannot make that table client calculation work because it gets attached to the member and when you change version it errors out. The only solution will be restricted measure and calculated measure for variance. I know its not ideal but I am not sure when we will have table level calculation working as dynamic.

Nikhil

entry.title: SAP Product Inspiration Series for Industries & Intelligent CX (Wave #6)
entry.content:
Hi everyone, toward the end of the year I'm totally excited to announce the 6th Wave of our Product Inspiration Series in which we dive into latest innovations across Intelligent CX from SAP, in conjunction with SAP S/4HANA, BTP, Business AI and industry-tailored scenarios. Please check out our latest episodes below.

 

1. Digital Selling Workspace in SAP Sales Cloud V2 in Action

Punav Prabhudesai from the Sales Cloud Product Management at SAP provides an overview on the new Digital Selling Workspace. Watch this product inspiration video to learn about the functional capabilities of this user-centric workspace in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2 as well as our major innovations as part of the Intelligent CX portfolio from SAP.

The key highlights shared in this video are:
00:00 - Introduction
00:55 - Why Digital Selling Workspace in SAP Sales Cloud Version 2?
02:24 - What Are the Key Innovations in Digital Selling Workspace?
03:60 - How Can Customers Adopt the Digital Selling Workspace?
04:42 - Start of the Demo
05:01 - Demo: Digital Selling Workspace Overview
05:42 - Demo: Call Lists and Call Scripts
07:24 - Demo: Log a Call and Create Follow-Up Activities
09:31 - Demo: Manage Leads and Activities Effectively
11:57 - Demo: Manage Opportunities and Activities Effectively
14:43 - Demo: Manage Email Activities Effectively
15:30 - Demo: Adaptation Mode for Digital Selling Workspace
17:24 - Summary: Intelligent CX from SAP

 

2. SAP Commerce Cloud in Action

Marcus Schreiner from the Commerce Cloud Product Leadership Team at SAP provides an overview on SAP Commerce Cloud. Watch this product inspiration video to learn more about the latest innovations as well as the strategic direction for Composable Commerce as part of the Intelligent CX portfolio from SAP.

The key highlights shared in this video are:
00:00 - Introduction
00:58 - Why SAP Commerce Cloud?
02:06 - Which Scenarios does SAP Commerce Cloud offer?
03:38 - How can Customer Adopt SAP Commerce Cloud?
04:15 - Start of the Demo
08:52 - Summary: Intelligent CX from SAP

 

The following videos are still in process - but should be available in the coming weeks:

  • Quiq AI for SAP Intelligent CX
  • Extensibility of SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2 using NCLC Tools
  • Extensibility of SAP Sales and Service Cloud V2 with SAP BTP by using the example of ORBIS TEM
  • SAP Predictive Planning and Inventory Orchestration 
  • SAP Customer Checkout in Action
  • Partner Tactics in SAP Emarsys Customer Engagement

 

Curious to learn more about Intelligent CX from SAP? Then check out the other videos:  

Back to the Product Inspiration Overview  |  Wave I  |  Wave II  |  Wave III  |  Wave IV  |  Wave V

entry.title: Re: Eclipse - Where-Used functionality for ABAP CDS views not working
entry.content:
ABAP 7.58 doesn't have any business function SRIS_CODE_SEARCH and doesn't provide a where-used list between CDS views, only SAP GUI transaction SDDLAR works as shown here: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-discussions/where-used-functionality-for-abap-cds-views/m-p/13593220/highlight/true#M2026372.

EDIT: you mean SRIS_SOURCE_SEARCH. This one exists in ABAP 7.58 and deactivated by default. Be careful, activating it makes REPOSRC table grow from 10 to 100 GB (source: 3339157 - How to deactivate business function SRIS_SOURCE_SEARCH - SAP for Me).

entry.title: Re: Issue with Automatic Cancellation of Purchase Invoice in Intercompany Billing
entry.content: hi

entry.title: API Management vs Open Connectors
entry.content:
Technology Q&A

Dear all, as a background I am working on a project where we need to consume some API's and make them available in BTP. We had a meeting with the customers and as a solution we have presented them Integration Flows and API Management. We are not having experience with Integration Flows and API Management so we are currently on a learning curve and trying to learn more about them.

While initiating the project they wanted us to configure Open Connectors(on top of Integration Flows) in order to make their APIs externally visible and be able to be commercialized. 
When I did some research we found that the best option for this purpose was the API Management on top of Integration Flows - as I understood Open Connectors might not be available anymore in the future.

Now the customer is always asking what's the difference between them Open Connectors vs API Management. Can someone please help me to differentiate API Management from Open Connectors and show the advantages/disadvantages ?

Their goal is to have their APIs exposed to a marketplace, have external visibility and in future to be able to commercialize them...Can we help them reach their goal with API Management? Is there a place where the APIs can be exposed in the same way as Open Connectors?
Thanks in advance!

Is there any other configuration should I do or not?
After run the function lo_cl_somu_form_services->get_document(), then the exception was caught.

entry.title: Re: Regex help
entry.content:
Hi @aravind_indu ,

I'm not sure if lookaheads are working, but with a Perl like syntax it is look like this:

 

^(?=.{10,18}$)[a-zA-Z]{3,10}\.[a-zA-Z]{3,10}$

Regards,
Peter

entry.title: Re: Default values with abstract entity
entry.content: Hi, thank you but I am getting "Annotation 'UI.defaultValue' unknown"

entry.title: Re: Regex help
entry.content:
Using callout it is possible.

DATA lv_text TYPE string VALUE `Aravind.Induchudan is a mail id. This is great.`.
lv_text = lv_text && ` Sam.Tom is another mail id. Anirudh.Induchud is another mail`.


CLASS handle_regex DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    DATA lv_len TYPE i.
    INTERFACES if_abap_matcher_callout.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS handle_regex IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD if_abap_matcher_callout~callout.
    DATA lv_result TYPE if_abap_matcher_callout=>callout_result.
    IF callout_num EQ 1.
      lv_len = capture_last_len.
    ELSEIF callout_num EQ 2.
      lv_len = capture_last_len + lv_len.
      IF lv_len LT 10 OR lv_len GT 18.
        callout_result = IF_ABAP_MATCHER_CALLOUT=>c_callout_result-FAIL.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS demo_pcre DEFINITION.
  PUBLIC SECTION.
    CLASS-METHODS main.
ENDCLASS.

CLASS demo_pcre IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD main.
    DATA(regex) = cl_abap_regex=>create_pcre(
      pattern = `(\w{3,10})(?C1)(\.)(?C3)(\w{3,10})(?C2)` ).

    DATA(matcher) = regex->create_matcher( text = lv_text ).

    DATA(handler) = NEW handle_regex( ).
    matcher->set_callout( handler ).
    DATA(lv_result) = matcher->match( ).

    TRY.
        CALL METHOD matcher->find_all
          RECEIVING
            matches = DATA(lt_result).
        LOOP AT lt_result INTO DATA(ls_result).
          WRITE: /, lv_text+ls_result-offset(ls_result-length).
        ENDLOOP.
      CATCH cx_sy_matcher.
    ENDTRY.

    cl_demo_output=>display( ).
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  demo_pcre=>main( ).

entry.title: Re: BAPI to request the stock in a storage location
entry.content: How its give material stocks i am not cleared. BAPI_GOODSMVT_GETDETAIL one is used to get material documents wise data.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Automatic Cancellation of Purchase Invoice in Intercompany Billing
entry.content: hi thank you for your comment, I understand it. If this is not the standard behavior, could you please advise on how to configure the system to automatically reverse the related purchase invoice when the sales invoice is canceled? Is it possible to use IDOC customization to automate this process?

entry.title: Re: Adding the Logo in the SAP BTP Adobe Form in S/4 publ...
entry.content:
Hi Swarup,
if you want to embed a logo to the template directly, make sure the embed image data option is checked for the image field in Adobe LiveCycle Designer for sap solutions.

If you want to embed images during runtime, create a new data field of type abap.rawstring(0) (internally represents xstring) and fill it. The reuse class for receiving xml data tree (https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/rap-data-services-for-print-forms?version=Cloud) will resolve the xstring to base64 automatically.

After this, create a binding to this data field in your image field in Adobe LiveCycle Designer.

Best regards
Pascal

entry.title: Re: Unable to activate Contract
entry.content: If those are 'warning' message, try ignore them pressing Enter?

entry.title: Re: How to connect On Premise SAP to BTP using SAP Cloud Connector
entry.content:
Hi All,

 

I'm trying to connect SAP Cloud connector (installed on my MAC - a portable version) to SAP BTP Trail version - Subaccount.I tried to add the subaccount in the cloud connector under "define sub-account". I have given the region , sub account ID , email and password. But I'm getting error when I click on Finish button

Error:

"Proxy cannot be reached"

I tried with the other sub account details and still getting the above error.

Just want to know what user should be given under "subaccount user" while adding the subaccount.

I have given the user details (with which I have created the subaccount), but still above error occurs.

Please suggest how to fix the error.

Regards,

Satish

entry.title: INVOIC02 iDod with Down Payment and BillingPlan
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

we are integrating SAP SD Billing with Peppol Network/ eInvoice 3.0. Therefore we are using INVOIC02 iDoc as basis. We have Business cases with downpayment/ billingplan and final invoices. Infact the final invoice has serious issues in integration as the 'amouts payed' is designed as separate billing item which "understands" the eInvoice Standard as own item. Besides of that eInvoice wants to have the "amounts payed" on header level which is not compatible to the billg plan design is SAP with AZWR conditiontype. 

Any ideas? Is there an extension/ functionmodue available to implement this changes in iDOc or a different messagetype?

 

Regards

Andreas

entry.title: INVOIC02 iDod with Down Payment and BillingPlan
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

we are integrating SAP SD Billing with Peppol Network/ eInvoice 3.0. Therefore we are using INVOIC02 iDoc as basis. We have Business cases with downpayment/ billingplan and final invoices. Infact the final invoice has serious issues in integration as the 'amouts payed' is designed as separate billing item which "understands" the eInvoice Standard as own item. Besides of that eInvoice wants to have the "amounts payed" on header level which is not compatible to the billg plan design is SAP with AZWR conditiontype. 

Any ideas? Is there an extension/ functionmodue available to implement this changes in iDOc or a different messagetype?

 

Regards

Andreas

entry.title: Re: Flow function: "Set repeated item value" suddenly not working
entry.content: i am returning from TechEd in UK, but I will investigate on my return … but could you use the regular set variable flow function in the meantime?

entry.title: Outgoing payment approval using Bank payment Advice in SAP Business ByDesign do not go for approval
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,
 
Please we are currently having a challenge with Outgoing payment approval using Bank payment Advice in SAP Business ByDesign
 
The challenge is that, it does not go for approval even after all setup has been done. For proper context, the below are what has been setup:
 
 Under Business Configuration we made the following setup; 
 
 1. In Payment Approval - Outgoing Payments under  task: "Business Task Management for Payment and Liquidity Management", the Payment method availabe are:
a. Bank Transfer 
b. Check is:
 
We setup company payment methods for both Bank Transfer and Check as well as the threshold amount. 
 
 Under Application and User Management - Employee Work Distribution we made the following  setup;
 
 2. "Approver for Liquidity Management" and  "Approver for Payables"
 
We set up Rule as All, parameter as company and we defined a user responsible for approval, validity is active as the Rule valid To is set to "unlimited"
 
 After the setup has been completed, on making payment on supplier invoice in the system, it  only goes for approval when "New Outgoing Bank Transfer" is used to do the transaction
But using New Bank Payment Advice it does not go for approval!
 
Please help point out any issue we may be missing out, as to why Bank Payment advice skips the payment approval workflow.

entry.title: Export multiple accesses data of a user on a course
entry.content:
Hello,

we"d like to know if it is possible to export, by using Plateau, People Analytics, API, date and time of the single accesses of a user into a course.

From an initial analysis performed, no tables were found that export each access made by a user on an online course for example.

Thank you in advance

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 TB report BP display filtered by GL Accounts
entry.content:
Hello! thank you for your answer. General Ledger has only one issue. It does not display ending balance for every customer.

I have attached screenshots.

First one is selection criteria, second one is general ledger. it displays opening balance in "Cumulative Balance LC" field in light grey color. then in Debit/Credit displays turnover for selected period and ending balance is not displayed. 

Report summarizes totals for all customers, in my case, I have displayed only one customer and total of this report is correct, but in real life, customer will choose several business partners, the bottom of this page will contain all data

entry.title: Re: Inventory Revaluation with Inventory debit/credit typ...
entry.content:
The item is valuated by Standard Cost method? This is an important information, because if that is the case, you cannot change inventory value in any way other that with revaluation document of Price Change type.

What you are trying to do is add value to existing stock, but since the item is valued as standard cost, it is not allocating any value to item itsefl. And that is the reason you don't see the transaction in inventory audit report, because it didn't actually affect inventory.

From accounting perspective, this should also be obvious to you when you check journal entry of revaluation document. Usually, negative revaluation should be posted to gl decrease account / inventory account. But your journal entry is posted to gl decrease account /variance account (maybe you got confused because variance and inventory account are the same in account determination, they shouldn't be).

entry.title: Enabling Credit management check in Solution order
entry.content:
Business Problem

A professional service industry wants to have a credit check enabled for Customer Projects in S4 HANA.

But the Customer project object by default does not support the Credit check functionality .

Approach

Solution order document supports the creation of projects as part of its standard functionality.

So if a credit check can be performed on the the net value of the solution order , it would allow us to understand the credibility of the Consumer requesting for the service or products .

Solution

Implement the BADI CRMS4_SERV_CHECK_BEFORE_SAVE   to perform a credit check on solution order.

If the document type is “SOLO” , then proceed with checking the  credit details.  

Currently SAP credit management is not providing any API to get the Credit score from the  S4 system .

Hence in public cloud we will query the CDS view I_CreditManagementAccount business partner as the sold to party function .

 

Credit management required settings

Open the application maintain business partner in the change mode and in the  in BP Role  assign the role UKM000 – SAP Credit management.

In the credit profile maintain the rule : SAP All External agencies & Int Scoring and also maintain a risk class

 

Also maintain the credit segment data

 

Manage Credit account.

Maintain the limit in the credit segment

 

Implementation details

In the public cloud use the custom logic app to create a BADI implementation .

Click on open code editor to write the logic for the credit check

 

The field serviceheader-servicedocumenttype , serviceheader-soldtoparty , serviceheader-transactioncurrency provides the current document type , the business partner and creditsegmentcurrency respectively to query on the CDS view i_creditmanagementaccount.

 

 

 

Please note: Knowing the fact that service-related document currently do not send any warning message to the UI passed from a BADI implementation the message type have to be of type error.  And also the solution order will be saved in the system with an error message. Further processing for the document will be possible only when the error is resolved.

entry.title: Re: Admin Proxy in the New Learning Experience
entry.content: Hi, the linked KBA has been removed. Is this behavior connected to the issue that admins can not see the new learning experience at all when acting as proxy?

entry.title: Re: Public Dimension Table Import Job Error
entry.content: Hi, we had an issue in one hierarchy of the public dimension too. Some members were not in the hierarchy. Once we added them manually the import jobs started refreshing successfully again. Thanks

entry.title: Re: Regex help
entry.content: This regex works also with POSIX regex.

entry.title: Re: Eclipse - Where-Used functionality for ABAP CDS views not working
entry.content:
I was able to find business function SRIS_CODE_SEARCH in transaction SFW5 and could successfully activate it. After activation, the error on the object search in Eclipse disappeared. However, I got further information. A colleague working on the same system as I am faces the same issue. However, the colleague for whom it works is on S/4HANA 2023 Initial Shipment. I'll upload the screenshots in a second. Thus, I wonder what is missing for 2023 SP02. Was the functionality taken out? And yes, SDDLAR works but it is slow and not that handy.

EDIT: You are correct with the business function. Sorry for the confusion. 

EDIT 2: Screenshots from the colleague where it works

entry.title: SAP BRIM and Partner Products like, e.g. SAP CM by DR - Product Availability Matrix?
entry.content:
SAP BRIM (former Hybris Billing Solution) 

I cannot find SAP BRIM and the related partner solutions like, e.g. SAP Converged Mediation in the official SAP PAM?

How is that possible, i mean, SAP offers two different deployment stacks (Private and Public Cloud), but there is not technical background information about which type of Cloud Provider is supported or which OS/DB/etc version is certified for certain deployment cases?

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Setting Default Values for Create in ABAP RESTful app...
entry.content: I use Visual Studio Code. Where I have to insert the code snipplet? In which file?

entry.title: Adding Long Term Backup to existing Contract - What are the options?
entry.content:
A customer has a 5 year contract starting 31-03-2022.

The customer want by 01-01-2025 to upgrade with the option: 8011088 SAP long-term backup, yearly backup with 4-year retention, private edition

Is this possible?

The 4 year option goes beyond 5 year contract end date (30-03-2027)!

So is the correct option for the request to hire the 3 year option (to include the remaining 2 year plus 3 month) or is the 2 year option the correct option?

  • 8011086 SAP long-term backup, yearly cloud backup with 2-year retention, private edition
  • 8011087 SAP long-term backup, yearly cloud backup with 3-year retention, private edition
  • 8011088 SAP long-term backup, yearly backup with 4-year retention, private edition

Thanks,

 

RISE with SAP

entry.title: How can I delete SH from on sold-to and move the same SH under a different sold-to account
entry.content: We are merging some "duplicate sold to accounts". So I will use one single sold to instead of 2 separates (we sold different brands under different accounts. I need to move some of the ship-to accounts from the dormant/ duplicate account -> to the agreed single "one". How is that possible?

entry.title: Re: How to find the history of approved or rejected form...
entry.content:
Hello,

Once you have set up and run the process. You can manually monitor the transactions/item in the Control Tower -> Monitoring -> Process/Workflows. 

This is effective on debugging stage. To do it on live/running process, you can create Process Visibility Scenarios to create performance indicators and see process variables/attributes you assigned. 

I believe this could be the correct way of doing things. 

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-create-process-visibility..html

**********************

As an option, you may create a process/automation that will notify and logged all transactions - (this is dependent on the overall architecture of your Process/Automation.

entry.title: Re: Saprouter denies https after some time
entry.content: When the -D option is not used, saprouter makes a reversed DNS check. Perhaps this contradicts with the Linux DNS cache. On the other hand reverse DNS check may not always return the same host. Probably it is better to use -D.

entry.title: Re: Introducing the new Adaptation Editor experience for SAPUI5 adaptation projects
entry.content: Hi @Summitt12 Most of them yes. There are few legacy apps which do not (yet) fulfil the prerequisites for adaptation projects. You can find out in the Fiori Apps reference library, which has a dedicated section IMPLEMENTATION INFORMATION -> Extensibility

entry.title: Re: How to find ACTVT Activity field / value in program?
entry.content:
Dear Romy, can you explain me better your requirement?

Do you wanna insert a custom authority check in a custom program? Or do you only need to check which authorization value to assign to ACTVT field in a standard program?

entry.title: Data migration sequence for Subscription Billing
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

very soon we'll start data migration for Subscription billing objects and I would like some clarifications concerning the sequence specifically for products, rate plans, rate plan templates.

When data is created manually the order is the following: create a rate plan template, create a product and during or after product creation  assign/create a rate plan with reference to a rate plan template.

Looking at the Product API, I have the impression that the rate plan cannot be created at the same time as the product as the rate plan ID should be assigned to the product, but if the rate plan is not created yet the ID is not available. 

Could you please confirm if below sequence is correct ?
Is there a more efficient strategy (ex. create product + rate plan at the same time) ?

1. Create rate plan templates
2. Create rate plan with reference to previously created rate plan template ID
3. Create Product and assign previously created rate plan ID 

Thank you very much in advance.

entry.title: Replication Cost Center from S4 Hana (S4HCM version) to Employee Central
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I got the information that cost center replication from S4 Hana is not supported without SAP MDI (Master Data Integration) in latest S4 Hana for HCM.

So I would like to know or anyone can confirm if it is not supported any longer using SAP CPI?

If possible please providing any KBAs related, because I need to communicate this matter with our client.

Regards,
Reza

entry.title: Re: Regex help
entry.content:
Thanks, interesting to see a callout example. So, PCRE (since ABAP 7.55). I guess it's more an exercise than real life code, because testing the match length is a much shorter code:

FIND ALL OCCURRENCES OF PCRE '...' IN '...' RESULTS DATA(matches).
LOOP AT matches WHERE length BETWEEN 10 AND 18...
  ...
ENDLOOP.

More about PCRE/Callouts in ABAP: Modern Regular Expressions in ABAP - Part 1 - Intr... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Issue with Automatic Cancellation of Purchase Invoice in Intercompany Billing
entry.content:
Hi,

We will issue a credit memo for reversal through IDOC,

but sorry, I don’t know the detailed configuration of IDOC.

entry.title: Re: Solution Order - Subscription BAdI Extensibility
entry.content:
Hi Kangkana, please find here the link to the same question in Q&A: https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-q-a/creation-custom-references-in-subscription-item-via-badi-from-solution/qaq-p/13954116

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Regex help
entry.content: Thanks Peter

entry.title: ABAP Create Customers and vendors
entry.content:
Hello, 

I am looking for a way to create customers and vendors from ABAP code, and assign to Company Code, in a legacy EPR system (not S4), and BP concept is not activated.

I guess inserting records in LFA1/LFB1 and KNA1/KNB1 is not a good approach.

Is there an ABAP function to do that? I cannot find anything in Google.

Thanks, ILIAN

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354088411.html

entry.title: Re: SAP shutdown
entry.content: Hi

entry.title: Re: SAP shutdown
entry.content: We have the same problem in a client's production environment. Were you able to solve the issue? How did you fix it?. Thanks

entry.title: BAPI for Material storage location wise stock check
entry.content:
I need a BAPI in which i can check material storage location & plant wise current stock.

Right now i am using "BAPI_MATERIAL_AVAILABILITY" but here its give total stock but i need storage location wise stock.

Regards-

Rajib

entry.title: Re: How to conduct employee internal transfer to another...
entry.content:
Hello Julia,

Is this scenario happening in a S/4HANA system? I understand the employee has stopped working in the previous legal entity and is now working in the new merged legal entity. I cannot comment from a legal point of view, but I think we are talking about two different company codes. In that case, the best practices are setting the old personnel number to inactive (as the employee is not longer working in the previous company code), and hiring a new personnel number for the new legal entity. These two personnel numbers should be in a multiple assignment scenario so both personnel numbers are linked together. In that case, the system will understand these two personnel numbers refer to the same employee (same human person).

Regarding users, in S/4HANA we have a simplified process and, due to auditing requirements, we only allow to have one user per employee. So, even if you hire an additional new personnel number for this employee, the user from the first personnel number is still be valid and can be used (for example, business users for loggingin fiori launchpad, or the HR CDS Views will still return the user). You can also assign the same user ID to the second personnel number, but we do not support having different user id in each personnel number.

entry.title: Re: Focused Build Customer Extension Field Changes to Metadata Configuration and Process Type View,
entry.content:
Thanks for the quick reply @Imran05  so AXTSYS settings were in fact missing in production. I have just updated these and reset the cache via /IWFND/CACHE_CLEANUP (as explained in the other blog post).
Custom Fields are visible in /IWFND/GW_CLIENT but still it's not shown in the UI (browser cache has also been refreshed).
Do you have any other idea what else we can check? Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Moving/Rolling averages - SAC, Lumira, Eclipse Query...
entry.content:
Hi Bolaji,

thank you for you answer. I tried to understand what you mean and how you would do it yesterday, but I haven't got it to work. In the last weeks I also talked about this topic to a few people and all of them told me that it is not possible in the SAC. They told me the only option would be to calculate it in the BW Provider beforehand. Are you sure that is should be possible with the SAC?

Best regards,

Felix

entry.title: Re: Inventory Revaluation with Inventory debit/credit typ...
entry.content:
It is clear now.

If the method is Moving Average, Price Change & Inventory Debit/Credit are the same affect.

However, It is different in case of Standard Cost method. Price Change is the only way to change Standard Cost of an item and then affects to Audit Report.

entry.title: SPAU Notes for processing disappears after cancelling the implementation
entry.content:
We have recently upgraded our development system to EHP8 and currently in the SPAU phase.  When we are processing Notes, we double click Reimplementation.  Then when we need to further check the note details or an activation error occurs, we cancel in one of the pop-ups thinking we can go back to it.  But the note disappears from the list after.  Checking SNOTE, the note has status Incompletely Implemented and the note is still in table SMODILOG.  We have a work around with SE95 or SNOTE, but it is tedious.  Is this a known issue and is there a note about it to correct SPAU? 

I have searched here in the discussions and SAP support, turned up with none.

entry.title: User cannot display or create Service Request for all Systems/Installations
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am facing issues while creating Service Requests for ECS team.

When I try to create a Service Request, I only see 82 items for ECS. I don’t see anything for LB for inbound traffic.

But when my  does the same thing, he sees 117 items in ECS and he is able to see the LB for inbound traffic type request.

Thanks,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: User cannot display or create Service Request for all...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

We might remind you, like in SAP KBA 1282808 - What tasks is a Super, Cloud, or User Administrator S-user ID responsible for? - SAP for Me described, that every super administrator is responsible for the administration of S-users. See also About the User, Authorization and Administrator Concept. Super administrators have all authorizations for all applications in the SAP for Me portal at the highest possible level, i.e. usually for all installations under the customer number (and in case of a Customer Center or Expertise for all customer numbers under the Customer CoE). 

Most of our authorization objects can be assigned on Debtor, Installation or COE Level. You should always consider that your user might have only on a lower level authorizations assigned. Probably the other S-user is assigned on Debtor Level, which gives him more power, then your user.

In order to have full usage on the Service Request Application we highly recommend you to have all 3 Objects assigned the best on Debtor Level: 

Create Service Requests                     
Display Service Requests                 
Create Billable Service Requests          

SAP KBA 1282808 - What tasks is a Super, Cloud, or User Administrator S-user ID responsible for? - SAP for Me

SAP KBA 3463116 - Missing template(s) when creating service request - SAP for Me

SAP KBA 3381743 - How to maintain authorizations and Super Administrators for partner customer numbers - SAP for Me

Best wishes 

SAP for Me Product Support

Dragana

entry.title: Re: BAPI for Material storage location wise stock check
entry.content: How did you call the BAPI, since its signature already contains the STGE_LOC parameter?

entry.title: Ramp up percentage

With ramping up, what percentage of the final FUE size can be used. is this information available in any documentation.

Is it possible on the customer side to build a Linux based docker image. It should be supported for .Net and C#.

BR

Gaurav

entry.title: Re: PRAT1, PRAT2 Fields are not showing at MDG UI Level upon adding to Data model BP_SALES.

entry.title: Login to SAC without HANA Cloud Service Account, soly with SAP Account
entry.content:
Hi,

SAC does not seem to be enterprise ready :

Requirement:
Want to log in into SAC without a Hana Cloud Service Account for each and any SAP User
AND
Want to have a nationalized SAC Profile EN/DE according to the SAP Users need.

Current situation:
-SAC
-CORS to S4 / Embedded BW
-SAC Storries soly based on Live CORS Connections

Plan:
-200 existing SAP Accounts, englisch and german, to access the SAC CORS Live Data Connection based storries,
-add on: some SAP Users require EN SAC Formatting and EN SAC Labels, 

Dont want:
-a HANA Cloud Service Account, SAP Userid, for any of these 200 SAP Accounts at all:


-to complicated for the Users 
-to time consumeing each and any time

Might be willing to have:
-Introduce Active X SSO
Or:
-Introduce Azure ID

Whats the solution ?

Thank You !

Best Martin

entry.title: Re: ABAP Create Customers and vendors
entry.content:
Are the VMD_EI_API and CMD_EI_API classes already available in your version (?)

Else look at LSMW

  • objects 0040 and 0050 (report to create BDC)
  • Reorded Batch input
  • IDOC CREMAS, DEBMAS (+ADRMAS)

entry.title: Re: RAP Odata V4 APIs from S4HANA public cloud not visibl...
entry.content:
I got the answer.

Setup the connection as mentioned in the Tutorials

Connect SAP Business Application Studio and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, ABAP Environment | SAP Tutorials

 

Also, follow the attachment in SAP Note -  3063160 - All catalog service requests failed for system: <destination URL>/<sap_client>. OData version(s): V2, V4. - SAP for Me

In the attachment in above SAP Note, notice the option selected in DataSource (It is 'Connect to an OData Service', for OData V2, we generally select 'Connect to a system') and in the OData Service (the hostname is created with <destination_name>.dest )

OData SAP Business Application Studio #S4HANACloud, ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model

entry.title: Need to update price with validity period at mass level in agreement (ME32L)
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

 

We are precure material through Agreement ,  now price on material have been changed and there are more that 90 materials in an agreement and there are 10 agreements. It is very hard to change validity with price for all materials. So, we request you to please provide us solution so we can update new price for the same agreements for a new time span. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Need to update price with validity period at mass lev...
entry.content:
There is an App that can be used to update the Price in Mass:

Price Change Scheduling Agreements

With this app, you as a purchaser can change a supplier’s prices in info records, contracts, or scheduling agreements, providing they have time-dependent conditions.

Key Features

  • Make changes to the gross price.

  • Change the prices relating to a condition type.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/7018ae1367584a62aa32d50b39e2920e.html?version=2408.500

 

Other apps which can be interesting for mass edit:

Schedule Update of Price for Purchasing Documents

With this app, as a configuration expert, you can schedule jobs to determine and update prices relating to the Purchase Contract Item, Scheduling Agreement Item, and Purchasing Info Record. You can select a job template, set the required parameters and schedule the job

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/2a5b355e439b468fabf1c9b1c4504557.html?version=2408.500

 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/f07fb65334e6b54ce10000000a174cb4.html?version=2408.500

Thanks 

Best regards

entry.title: Re: ABAP Create Customers and vendors
entry.content:
Have you tried API classes?

  • VMD_EI_API - for Vendors
  • CMD_EI_API - for Customers

entry.title: Re: Defining Custom Subscription Parameters in Multitenant SAP BTP CF Applications
entry.content:
Hi Brian,

thanks for this blog post. Unfortunately, it seems that defining the form view is no longer possible via the mentioned _show_form_view parameter. I copied your solution and only get the JSON view, which is of course missing the entered descriptions and enums, etc.

Are you aware of a new way for defining the form view?

Best Regards
Sven

entry.title: Re: EWM How to delete incorrectly created Party Entitled...
entry.content: EDIT: Ticket raised to SAP.. only solution was to delete the error entry directly from the table /SAPAPO/MATLWH

entry.title: Re: How to create a value help for custom and released domain fixed values?
entry.content:
For everyone wondering why it doesn't work:

You have to enter the name of your Data Element for "loclaConstant" NOT the Domain Name here:

      @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: 
      [{ entity: { name : 'ZI_DOMAIN_FIX_VAL' , element: 'low' } ,
         additionalBinding: [{ element: 'domain_name',
                               localConstant: 'DATA_ELEMENT_NAME', usage: #FILTER }]
                               , distinctValues: true
                               }]

 

This is really confusing, I don't know why the coding examples are not fixed by using the right terms.

(Everyone having the same name for the domain and the data element is not affected obvsly)

entry.title: Authentication using JWT in SAP API Management
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are in a process of implementing OAuth(client credentials based) authentication to API proxies using the JWT verify policy in SAP API Management as described in this post. We used EntraID as IdP. We have been able to successfully implement and test it, but have an observation though. 

We noticed during testing that token fetched for app registered for Dev environment is also working for authenticating with API proxy in SAP APIM Test environment, which is most likely because both the APIs (dev and test) are registered in a common EntraID. 
But is this how it should work? Is there a way to ensure that token fetched for API in one environment does not works for APIs in another environment?

Any inputs will be appreciated. Thanks!

Regards,

Faisal

entry.title: Re: Error when checking package SMLT - Language installation
entry.content:
After open a SAP Case and discuss the issue internally this is the official answer from SAP Side: 

 

Your system is kind of unique in a way that the EHP8 DVD doesn't fit, as your system is not an EHP8 system for ERP 6.0, but an EHP8 system for SEM-BW (Note 0001927083). Hence the missing components regarding the EHP8 ERP6.0 language DVD. You have to use the NetWeaver language DVD instead. So, the best fit from Note 0000330104 is:

NetWeaver 7.50

CA:

                    Material Numbers / Available Languages:

                    Shared BlueRay Media, Material Number updated frequently, not listed here any more, please see support.sap.com

                    AR, BG, CA, CS, DA, DE, EL, EN, ES, ET, FI, FR, HE, HI, HR, HU, IT, JA, KK, KO, LT, LV, NL, NO, PL, PT, RO, RU, SH, SK, SL, SV, TH, TR, UK, VI, ZF, ZH

                    Language Transport Names:

                    SAPKL<ISO>750CANW7500X0 ..X9 for exchange export DE and EN

                    SAPKL<ISO>750CANW7500F9 for all languages (original texts included)

                    NetWeaver 7.50 includes:

SAP_BASIS 750:   on SP00 level

SAP_ABA 750:   on SP00 level

SAP_GWFND 750:   on SP00 level

SAP_UI 750:   on SP00 level

ST-PI 740: SAPK-74001INSTPI

SAP_BW 750:   on SP00 level

 

So basically, we need to use the DVDs based on NW 7.5 from Software Download SAP Page: 51050829_LNG1_part1 and 51050829_LNG1_part2. With this I can continue with the installation of languages successfully. 

 

Regards,

Chan.

entry.title: OBP: RTI after system copy from S/4
entry.content:
We have copied the productive system to the test system of S/4, it is connected to IBP OBP with a Real Time integration.

Unfortunately, this no longer worked properly. First, we deactivated and reactivated the integration profiles to trigger the initial data transfer. Unfortunately without success. Then I tried to recreate the RTI profile, which also did not have the desired effect. When I run the reconciliation job, the system recognizes that there is a difference, where I can trigger that, for example, Planned ORders come to IBP but not from IBP to S/4 and back again.

There is no error message that the transfer is not possible.
The communication arrangements also show that the connection between the two systems is correct.

Outbound is periodic, it only recognizes once a day that there are new planned orders to be transferred to s/4. After that there is no log header

entry.title: Partner function in equipment.
entry.content:
In SAP Public cloud, we have configured new partner function (Install At), the same getting determined in customer master and sales order. While creating delivery and doing PGI, we link serial number to the material (Extras - Serial Numbers - assign the respective serial number).
Need help with the requirement is to have this partner function populated in the partner function for the equipment and should be visible in IE03.

entry.title: Re: Profitability and Cost Allocation leveraging Dynamic Rule Maintenance
entry.content:
Hello, Jens.
Could you clarify please which option makes the rule dynamic. This is not explained in the post or in the help. We can only guess that empty selection makes the rule dynamic maybe. But if that is the case it's not clear why a dynamic (meaning editable) rule might not contain any selection by default. Is there some explicit option for this feature like a checkbox? just we don't see it.
Thank you.

entry.title: SPAU inactive objects of other user appears in my activation list
entry.content:
Our system has been upgraded to EHP8 and currently in SPAU phase.  While we are processing Notes, when I am activating after reimplementation, other developers SPAU inactive objects appear in my activate list and I cannot deselect them because the row selections are disabled.  Then if their objects have an error, it blocks me from activating my object.  It is just problematic because we need to process many objects in a limited time and I didn't experience these issues in previous upgrades.  Is it because we are saving in 1 SPAU transport request?

I have searched for OSS notes for this issue but I have found none so far.  Any advice on this?  Would also like to know what is the cause to this?

entry.title: Quick Data Cleanup in SAP Commerce
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Commerce generates operational data such as audit logs, job logs from cronjobs, job history, and more during regular use. Over time, this data can build up, consuming disk space and impacting performance. While periodic cronjobs are recommended for ongoing cleanup, sometimes a quick, one-time cleanup is necessary — for example, before a migration or to free up disk space. In this article, we’ll show you how to clean up this data efficiently using tools like Impex statements, Groovy scripts, and SQL commands.

Orphaned Types and Orphaned Media Files

Orphaned Types take up space in the database, and Orphaned Media Files can take up storage space on disk. Both can be easily cleaned up by going to HAC > Maintenance > Cleanup.

For a detailed guide on safely cleaning up orphaned objects, including how to handle them in CCv2, check out my other article - Orphaned Objects in SAP Commerce.

Media Items

Some types of media items in SAP Commerce become unnecessary over time and can safely be removed to free up system resources. A key benefit of SAP Commerce is that when a media item is deleted—whether through Backoffice, an Impex REMOVE statement, or a Groovy script — the associated physical file is also automatically deleted. This holds true for both on-premise setups and CCv2, where media files are stored in Azure Blob Storage.

However, if media records are deleted directly via SQL commands (not recommended), the underlying files will not be removed, resulting in orphaned files. If this happens, you can clean up these orphaned files using the “Delete Orphaned Media Files” procedure described in the previous chapter.

Two specific types of media items that often accumulate over time are cronjob logs and Impex media files, both of which can be cleaned up efficiently to save space.

Cronjob Log Files

SAP Commerce stores cronjob log files as media items with associated physical files. These logs are automatically deleted when their parent cronjob is removed. However, batch-deleting them separately in one operation can optimize the cleanup process and free up disk space faster.

Impex Media

Impex media are the files attached to Impex cronjobs, typically containing the scripts executed during the job. Unlike cronjob logs:
  • Impex media are not automatically deleted when their parent cronjob is removed.
  • They do not reference their parent cronjobs directly, so they remain in the system indefinitely, often accumulating in large numbers.
Impex media files are only used in Impex cronjobs. While they may have references in other types, those also ultimately lead back to Impex cronjobs. Therefore, it is generally safe to delete Impex media files, as most Impex cronjobs are completed, one-time processes. However, if you have recurring Impex cronjobs, you should carefully assess whether these files need to be retained.

Deleting Media Items

Timing the Cleanup

When performing a cleanup, it’s best to ensure the system is operating normally with no active issues or investigations. Cronjob logs and Impex media can be critical for troubleshooting, so avoid deleting them if you’re in the middle of resolving a problem. Also, schedule the cleanup during periods of low system load. For B2B systems, this often means weekday evenings or weekends. For B2C systems, you may need to run the cleanup late at night, considering your customers’ time zones to minimize disruptions.

Cleanup in Batch Mode

Deleting unnecessary media items, such as cronjob log files and impex media, is best done in batch mode. This allows the system to handle cleanup in a single operation, which is often faster and more efficient than removing items one by one. Please note that for this reason, we can't leave the "recent" items in the system and delete all the old ones, because then we won't be able to use the impex one-liner script below and will resort to removing items one-by-one, which will take longer and will not be as "quick" as this article promises.

To assess how many cronjob log files currently exist, use this query:

 

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {LogFile}

 

To delete all cronjob log files, use the following script:

 

REMOVE LogFile[batchmode=true];itemtype(code)[unique=true]
;LogFile

 

Once you launch the deletion of all job logs, SAP Commerce will create an ImpEx cronjob to handle the operation. You can monitor the progress of the deletion through this cronjob - when the deletion starts, you’ll see a log entry similar to the following:

 

Starting import synchronous using cronjob with code=00008MLZ
Starting multi-threaded ImpEx cronjob "ImpEx-Import" (8 threads)

 

The cronjob code, in this example 00008MLZ, will be unique to your system.

Open Backoffice, search for the cronjob using its code (e.g., 00008MLZ), and monitor its progress. Once the cronjob status shows as “COMPLETED”, the deletion has succeeded. To verify, rerun your initial query to count the number of JobLog entries:

 

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {LogFile}

 

If the deletion was successful, the result should be 0.

Please note that depending on the number of cronjob log files in your system, the process can take hours or even days - so be prepared for that. This will still be faster than removing these items with a cronjob one by one. From time to time, you can check how many items are removed thus far, by doing the "SELECT COUNT(*)" from the type you are cleaning up.

For Impex media, follow the exact same procedure as for cronjob logs, replacing LogFile with ImpExMedia in the querie and the script above.

Cleanup with a Groovy Script

When you need to clean up old items while keeping recent ones, you can use a Groovy script. The script processes deletions in batches, logs progress to both the console and system logs, and supports flexible date cutoffs. Logging is useful since the script may run for a long time, and your HAC session might time out. Logs will let you monitor progress even if your session disconnects.

You can find the script in the hybris-utils repository on GitHub. The cleanup process in the script is not limited to cronjob log files, but can be applied to any type or obsolete items in SAP Commerce where such items no longer serve a purpose and are safe to delete.

Sample logs:

 

Generated Query for LogFile (cutoff = 1Y 6M): SELECT {PK} FROM {LogFile} WHERE {creationtime} <= '2022-06-01 14:52:13'
Total LogFile items to delete (cutoff: 2022-06-01 14:52:13): 4,000
Deleted 1,000 LogFile items in this batch. Remaining: 3,000 (25.00% completed). Estimated time to complete: 15 minutes (or at 03:45 PM EST).
Deleted 1,000 LogFile items in this batch. Remaining: 2,000 (50.00% completed). Estimated time to complete: 10 minutes (or at 03:40 PM EST).
Deleted 1,000 LogFile items in this batch. Remaining: 1,000 (75.00% completed). Estimated time to complete: 5 minutes (or at 03:35 PM EST).
Deleted 1,000 LogFile items in this batch. Remaining: 0 (100.00% completed). Cleanup completed: 4,000 LogFile items deleted.
Obsolete items deletion script completed at: 03:30 PM EST
Total execution time: 20 minutes

 

For detailed usage and instructions, visit Hybris Obsolete Items Cleanup.

Stopping and Resuming the Cleanup

If the cleanup process is taking too long and needs to be paused, you have a couple of options depending on how the cleanup is running:

  1. For a Cronjob:

    You can set the corresponding cronjob status to “ABORTED” and then restart the servers. After the restart, the cronjob will no longer be running but will have completed part of the cleanup. When you’re ready to continue, you can restart the same cronjob to pick up where it left off.

  2. For a Groovy Script:

    To abort a Groovy script that’s running a cleanup operation, simply restart the server. The script will terminate immediately, though any changes already completed (e.g., deleted records) will remain applied. You can launch the script at a later time to resume from where it left off.

Cronjobs

Some cronjobs in the system are essential and must remain intact—this includes scheduled cronjobs with triggers. These should never be deleted. However, many completed cronjobs, such as those created during ad-hoc operations like ImpEx imports (e.g., via HAC) or catalog synchronizations, can usually be deleted once they are no longer needed.

In most systems, a regular maintenance procedure might retain a week or a month’s worth of these cronjobs to allow for potential investigations. However, since we’re performing a quick cleanup during a time when no investigations are ongoing, we can safely delete all cronjobs that are no longer required.

Step 1: Identify Cronjob Types

To determine which types of cronjobs exist and how many of each type are in the system, run the following query:

 

SELECT 
    {job.code} AS "Job Type", 
    COUNT({cronjob.pk}) AS "Number of CronJobs", 
    MAX({cronjob.modifiedTime}) AS "Latest Execution Time"
FROM 
    {CronJob AS cronjob 
     JOIN Job AS job 
     ON {cronjob.job} = {job.pk}} 
WHERE 
    NOT EXISTS (
        {{ SELECT 1 FROM {Trigger AS trigger} WHERE {trigger.cronJob} = {cronjob.pk} }}
    ) 
GROUP BY 
    {job.code} 
ORDER BY 
    COUNT({cronjob.pk}) DESC

 

This query will provide a table similar to the following:

Job Type

Number of CronJobs

Latest Execution Time

ImpEx-Export

154

2024-11-30 18:45:00

solrIndexerHotUpdateJob

47

2024-11-30 15:20:00

CatalogVersionSyncJob

32

2024-11-29 09:10:00

Step 2: Delete Specific Cronjob Types

If you decide to delete all cronjobs of a specific type, such as "ImpEx-Export", use the following Impex statement:

 

REMOVE CronJob[batchmode=true];job(code)[unique=true]
;ImpEx-Export

 

Monitoring the Deletion Process

Deleting cronjobs can take some time because, in addition to the cronjob itself, the process also removes associated items, such as:

  • Job Histories

  • Job Logs
  • Log Files (including cleaning up related files from disk and database)

To make the process faster, it is strongly recommended to delete cronjob log files before deleting the cronjobs themselves. This reduces the cleanup burden during the cronjob deletion process.

If you want to monitor the progress of the deletion, you can use a SELECT COUNT(*) query to check how many cronjobs of the specified type are still in the system. Here’s the query for ImpEx-Export:

 

SELECT COUNT(*)
FROM {CronJob AS cronjob
      JOIN Job AS job
      ON {cronjob.job} = {job.pk}}
WHERE {job.code} = 'ImpEx-Export'

 

Run this query periodically to see how many cronjobs remain, until the count reaches 0.

Business Processes

We deal with business processes in a similar way to how we handled cronjobs.

Step 1: Identify Business Process Types

Issue the following FlexibleSearch query to determine the number of business processes by type:

 

SELECT 
    {processDefinitionName} AS "Process Type", 
    COUNT({pk}) AS "Number of Processes", 
    MAX({modifiedTime}) AS "Latest Update Time"
FROM 
    {BusinessProcess}
GROUP BY 
    {processDefinitionName}
ORDER BY 
    COUNT({pk}) DESC

 

This query will return a table similar to the following:

Process Type

Number of Processes

Latest Update Time

order-process

1023

2024-11-30 18:45:00

quote-process

245

2024-11-29 14:10:00

subscription-process

87

2024-11-28 09:10:00

Step 2: Delete Specific Business Processes

Once you’ve identified the types of business processes you want to delete, use the following Impex statement to remove all instances of a specific type, such as "order-process":

 

REMOVE BusinessProcess[batchmode=true];processDefinitionName[unique=true]
;order-process

 

This statement deletes all business processes of type "order-process" in batch mode for efficiency.

Step 3: Monitor the Deletion Process

To track the progress of the deletion, you can use a count query to check how many business processes of the specified type remain:

 

SELECT COUNT(*)
FROM {BusinessProcess}
WHERE {processDefinitionName} = 'order-process'

 

Run this query periodically to verify that the count decreases as the deletion progresses. The process is complete when the count reaches 0.

Tables that can be truncated

Certain tables in SAP Commerce, such as Solr index operation table, stored HTTP sessions, and audit data tables, can be truncated directly in the database. Truncation is the fastest method for deleting data and is ideal when the rows are not referenced elsewhere in the system.

When to Use Truncation

  • Safe to truncate: Tables that are standalone and not referenced by other items, such as:
    • Solr index operation table (table SolrIndexOperation)
    • Stored HTTP sessions (table StoredHttpSessions)
    • Audit data tables (if audit data is not needed or has been archived) (tables ending with some number + "sn")
  • Do not truncate: Tables that have dependencies, such as:
    • Media items (which reference underlying files)
    • Cronjobs and business processes (referenced by orders, workflows, etc.)

Important Notes on Audit Data

Audit data often occupies a significant portion of database space — potentially up to 90% if enabled for a long time. Before truncating audit tables:

  1. Turn off audit data generation to prevent new data from accumulating immediately after the cleanup.
  2. Refer to my article on audit data for guidance on identifying audit tables: Audit Data and Audit Logging in SAP Commerce.

For example, to truncate the solr index operation table:

 

TRUNCATE TABLE SolrIndexOperation;

 

Steps for Truncating Tables

  1. Perform a backup:

    • Take a full database and media backup before truncating any tables.

    • Ensure you execute truncation during low-load times for minimal impact.

  2. Identify the target table:
    • For audit tables, refer to the linked article above.
    • For non-audit tables, check the items.xml file. The deployment element specifies the corresponding database table for the item.
  3. Execute the truncation:
    • Use the following SQL command in your database: TRUNCATE TABLE <table_name>;
    • Note: If you are hosted in CCv2, then you can issue a ticket to SAP so their DBA does the truncation for you.
  4. Clear caches:

    • Use HAC to clear the cache, or

    • Restart Hybris for a complete refresh.

Why Truncate?

Truncation is significantly faster than traditional deletion because it bypasses row-by-row checks and constraints, provided the table is standalone and not referenced by other entities. However, this method must be used carefully, as truncating a referenced table can break referential integrity and lead to system instability or errors.

File System Cleanup

File system cleanup is an important step to reclaim disk space during a quick cleanup. Focus on removing unnecessary files, such as Hybris logs, old hot folder items, and unused Azure storage files in CCv2.

  1. Hybris Logs:
    • Delete any old log files generated by SAP Commerce.
    • Consider automating this process by setting up a scheduled job to clean up logs older than a certain date.
  2. Hot Folders:
    • Clear out old files in hot folders that are no longer being processed or required.
    • For recurring processes, establish an automated cleanup routine to avoid manual intervention.
  3. Azure Storage (CCv2):
    • If your system is hosted in CCv2, review and clean up unused or outdated files in Azure Storage in Cloud Hot Folders.
    • Use Azure Storage Explorer or automated scripts to identify and delete unnecessary files.

Best Practices for File System Cleanup

  • Plan Cleanup During Low-Load Times:
    • Perform file system maintenance during off-peak hours to minimize the impact on active operations.
  • Backup Before Deleting:
    • Ensure critical files are backed up before deletion, particularly from hot folders or shared directories.
  • Automate the Process:
    • Use cron jobs, scheduled tasks, or scripts to automate routine cleanups.
  • Coordinate With Stakeholders:
    • Inform relevant teams before cleaning shared directories like hot folders, as other processes may depend on these files.

Conclusion

In this article, we outlined practical steps for quickly reclaiming disk and database space in SAP Commerce. By cleaning up obsolete database items, truncating non-referenced tables, and addressing file system clutter, you can optimize system performance and reduce storage costs. Regular maintenance and strategic cleanup ensure a more efficient and sustainable system over time.

entry.title: A/p Down Payment Invoice & A/p Invoice Query
entry.content:
Hi

  I need query to get A/p Down Payment Invoice linked with A/p Invoice.

Thanks
